title: feature: Strike-3 deploy-block enforcement (instrumentation-only iterations)
summary: Add SDK/runtime enforcement so a third forward-fix attempt for the same explicit bugClass is forced into instrumentation_only=true and deploy-blocked from algorithm-changing diffs unless an explicit audited --strike3-override is present.
acceptanceCriteria: Forward-fix attempt metadata has a typed, documented bugClass field and instrumentation_only state while preserving existing arbitrary metadata compatibility.
Two prior failed forward-fix attempts for the same bugClass cause the next attempt to be auto-flagged instrumentation_only=true.
Strike counts are derived only from journaled run/effect/result metadata and replay deterministically across repeated run:iterate calls.
instrumentation_only=true blocks configured algorithm-change file diffs during the gate unless an explicit --strike3-override flag with an auditable reason is provided.
Log-emission, env-flag, no-op-guard, and revert-only changes remain allowed during instrumentation-only iterations.
Algorithm-change patterns are configurable and defaults are narrow enough to avoid over-blocking docs, tests, and logging-only changes.
Override usage records actor, reason, bugClass, strike count, matched files, and timestamp in structured output or journal metadata.
Gate output uses the existing [gate] log stream style and includes actionable diagnostics for missing bugClass, matched algorithm files, and override requirements.
The current process validation surface is wired correctly: add a documented check:processes alias or integrate the check into validate:processes/CI without breaking existing validation.
Docs and CLI references describe bugClass, instrumentation_only, strike-3 deploy-block behavior, configurable patterns, and --strike3-override audit semantics.
Targeted runtime, replay, gate, CLI/script, docs, build, test, metadata, and diff hygiene verification passes or any unrelated failures are documented with exact evidence.
nonGoals: Do not re-open the broad #478 FORWARD_FIX_STRIKE_BUDGET design.
Do not add a database, external service, daemon, or mutable working-tree-derived strike store.
Do not block documentation, tests, log-only instrumentation, env flags, no-op guards, or revert-only changes merely because instrumentation_only=true.
Do not silently apply strike-3 override through undocumented environment variables.
Do not redesign unrelated runtime governance policy features beyond the narrow strike-3 deploy-block enforcement needed for #798.

processLibraryResearch.requestedRootStatus: absent in this checkout during planning

plannedPhases.name: Forward-fix metadata contract
plannedPhases.loop: RED tests for bugClass/instrumentation_only metadata, GREEN typed/persisted compatibility implementation, REFACTOR shared normalization, adversarial score until >=90.

plannedPhases.name: Deterministic strike-state derivation
plannedPhases.loop: RED tests for two failed fixes then strike-3 instrumentation_only, GREEN journal-derived strike helper, REFACTOR pure deterministic state, adversarial score until >=90.

plannedPhases.name: Instrumentation-only deploy-block gate
plannedPhases.loop: RED gate allow/deny tests, GREEN configurable file-pattern checker and process validation wiring, REFACTOR matching/config boundaries, adversarial score until >=90.

plannedPhases.name: Strike-3 override audit trail
plannedPhases.loop: RED override reason/audit tests, GREEN --strike3-override handling and structured audit output, REFACTOR audit payload normalization, adversarial score until >=90.

plannedPhases.name: Documentation and CI integration
plannedPhases.loop: RED docs/spec checks where available, GREEN docs/CLI/package script integration, REFACTOR wording/field-name consistency, adversarial score until >=90.

qualityGates: Every implementation phase starts by adding tests that fail for the intended missing behavior.
No production implementation task may proceed unless the red verification confirms failedForRightReason=true.
Green implementation is the minimum code needed to pass the phase tests.
Refactor occurs only after green tests and must re-run verification.
Adversarial review produces a 0-100 score with scoreBreakdown and blocking findings.
Each phase iterates until review.approved=true, verification.passed=true, and qualityScore >= 90.
Inter-phase checkpoints verify scope control, handoff readiness, exact command evidence, and no phase acceptance drift.
Final gate verifies all issue acceptance criteria, all non-goals, replay/idempotency behavior, override auditability, docs, build, tests, metadata, process validation, and diff hygiene.

breakpoints.when: The metadata placement or override CLI boundary is genuinely ambiguous after architecture mapping.
A phase cannot reach qualityScore >= 90 and verification.passed=true within maxIterationsPerPhase.
Final acceptance finds unresolved compatibility, replay, gate, or CI risk.

implementationPrBodyMustMention: Explicit bugClass and instrumentation_only metadata contract
TDD red/green/refactor verification and adversarial quality scores
